summary: Chinchilla (70B) matches Gopher’s compute budget while using 4× more training data, uniformly outperforming all prior LLMs including Gopher (280B) and GPT-3 (175B), and establishing that model size and training tokens should scale equally under a fixed compute budget.

blocks.content: We investigate the optimal model size and number of tokens for training a transformer language model under a given compute budget.

source_span.quote: We investigate the optimal model size and number of tokens for training a transformer language model under a given compute budget.

blocks.content: for compute-optimal training, the model size and the number of training tokens should be scaled equally: for every doubling of model size the number of training tokens should also be doubled.

source_span.quote: for compute-optimal training, the model size and the number of training tokens should be scaled equally: for every doubling of model size the number of training tokens should also be doubled.

blocks.content: We find that current large language models are significantly undertrained, a consequence of the recent focus on scaling language models whilst keeping the amount of training data constant.

source_span.quote: We find that current large language models are significantly undertrained, a consequence of the recent focus on scaling language models whilst keeping the amount of training data constant.

blocks.content: By training over 400 language models ranging from 70 million to over 16 billion parameters on 5 to 500 billion tokens, we find that for compute-optimal training, the model size and the number of training tokens should be scaled equally: for every doubling of model size the number of training tokens should also be doubled.

source_span.quote: By training over 400 language models ranging from 70 million to over 16 billion parameters on 5 to 500 billion tokens, we find that for compute-optimal training, the model size and the number of training tokens should be scaled equally: for every doubling of model size the number of training tokens should also be doubled.

blocks.content: We test this hypothesis by training a predicted compute-optimal model, Chinchilla, that uses the same compute budget as Gopher but with 70B parameters and 4× more more data.

source_span.quote: We test this hypothesis by training a predicted compute-optimal model, Chinchilla, that uses the same compute budget as Gopher but with 70B parameters and 4× more more data.

blocks.content: Chinchilla uniformly and significantly outperforms Gopher (280B), GPT-3 (175B), Jurassic-1 (178B), and Megatron-Turing NLG (530B) on a large range of downstream evaluation tasks.

source_span.quote: Chinchilla uniformly and significantly outperforms Gopher (280B), GPT-3 (175B), Jurassic-1 (178B), and Megatron-Turing NLG (530B) on a large range of downstream evaluation tasks.

blocks.content: This also means that Chinchilla uses substantially less compute for fine-tuning and inference, greatly facilitating downstream usage.

source_span.quote: This also means that Chinchilla uses substantially less compute for fine-tuning and inference, greatly facilitating downstream usage.

blocks.content: As a highlight, Chinchilla reaches a state-of-the-art average accuracy of 67.5% on the MMLU benchmark, greater than a 7% improvement over Gopher.

source_span.quote: As a highlight, Chinchilla reaches a state-of-the-art average accuracy of 67.5% on the MMLU benchmark, greater than a 7% improvement over Gopher.
